data.title: Ferrari N.V. purchased Euro 250 million of common shares in the first tranche of their buyback program. #Ferrari #RACE
data.description: In Maranello, Italy, on January 19, 2026, Ferrari N.V. (NYSE/EXM: RACE) has disclosed the purchase of Euro 250 million worth of common shares under the share buyback program initiated on December 16, 2025. This initial investment is part of a larger plan to repurchase approximately Euro 3.5 billion in shares by 2030, as outlined during the 2025 Capital Markets Day. The company will continue to buy back shares on Euronext Milan (EXM) on a daily basis as part of this ongoing program.

data.title: VIVOTEK enhances security in PathCare's laboratories in South Africa, improving staff and patient safety.
data.description: In Taipei on January 19, 2026, VIVOTEK announced the successful implementation of a cutting-edge security system in all of PathCare's pathology laboratories throughout South Africa. data.title: Veterinary Vaccines Market to reach $18.46 billion by 2031, growing at 7.2% CAGR.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida, on January 19, 2026, it was reported that the Veterinary Vaccines Market is anticipated to increase from approximately USD 13.02 billion in 2026 to USD 18.46 billion by 2031, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 7.2%. The market data includes 302 tables and 60 figures spread across 450 sources, as per MarketsandMarkets™ projections.

data.title: Alexa Translations strengthens UAE presence with legal partnerships and Trade Mission participation. Jan. 19, 2026.
data.description: Chosen as one of three Canadian firms to join Minister Sidhu's UAE Trade Mission in Dubai on January 19, 2026, Alexa Translations, a prominent provider of AI-driven language services, is solidifying its footprint in the area. This achievement highlights the company's commitment to expanding its reach and impact.

data.title: SBA Communications Corp announces tax reporting info for 2025 distributions. Consult tax advisor on specifics. Stock: SBAC, CUSIP: 78410G104, Record/Payment date: 3/13, Cash Distribution: per share, Ordinary Taxable Dividends: per share, Section 199A Dividends: per share.
data.description: SBA Communications Corporation (Nasdaq: SBAC) disclosed the tax reporting details for its 2025 distributions. The company advised stockholders to seek guidance from their tax advisors regarding their individual tax implications. The Common Stock CUSIP 78410G104, with the Ticker Symbol: SBAC, had a Record Date of 3/13 and a Payment Date. The Cash Distribution per share, Ordinary Taxable Dividends per share, and Section 199A Dividends per share were also specified for the shareholders' reference.

data.title: DeepHow launches PharmaCloud for pharmaceutical manufacturers to standardize, train, and verify operator execution. AI technology improves efficiency.
data.description: In Detroit, DeepHow introduced PharmaCloud, a GMP-compliant cloud platform for pharmaceutical and medical device companies. PharmaCloud utilizes advanced AI to standardize operator training, streamline execution, and ensure compliance with industry standards. This launch addresses the ongoing challenge faced by pharmaceutical manufacturers in balancing innovation and regulatory requirements. By leveraging AI-driven solutions, companies can enhance operator efficiency and maintain consistency in mission-critical tasks.

data.title: RapidWorks introduces Rapid Payments to speed up payment process for heavy equipment service providers.
data.description: A cutting-edge integrated payments platform has been launched in Boston on January 19, 2026, by RapidWorks. The platform aims to enhance cash flow, minimize administrative tasks, and streamline the collections process for heavy construction equipment service providers. Rapid Payments, the innovative solution, is designed to boost efficiency and convenience in payment processing.

data.title: Global DRaaS Market to reach $46,089.9 million by 2032, growing from $16,112.2 million in 2025 - MarketsandMarkets™.
data.description: In Delray Beach, Florida, as of January 19, 2026, data from MarketsandMarkets™ reveals significant growth in the global Disaster Recovery as a Service (DRaaS) Market. It is forecasted to increase from approximately USD 16,112.2 million in 2025 to a projected market size of USD 46,089.9 million by 2032. The substantial rise showcases the rapid expansion of the industry and underscores the increasing importance of DRaaS solutions in the market.
